sourceMovie.title: Election
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Election (2005) as a crime theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The crime and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Mesrine: Killer Instinct
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Election clicked for you, Mesrine: Killer Instinct (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Election?
faq.answer: Try Mesrine: Killer Instinct, Gaslight, Juice—each shares DNA with Election while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Election?
faq.answer: Election (2005) is best described as Crime, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Election part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Election Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
